title: Centrosolenia rubra

The classification of Centrosolenia rubra is further divided into various species and cultivars, each with unique characteristics and growth habits. Further research is needed to fully understand the taxonomy and classification of this plant.

intro: Centrosolenia rubra, also known as Solenopsis rubra, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, infections, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Centrosolenia rubra include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote well-being in traditional medicine.
